subject: Science
sample_standards: Your child understands that kidneys remove waste from the blood and turn it into urine, which is stored in the bladder.
Your child uses several ways to protect information when it is sent securely.
Your child can check if security tools like passwords and virus protection are being used correctly to keep information safe.
Your child can learn and explain how managing pests carefully can make indoor and outdoor places better.
Your child can name and explain the three main goals of security: keeping information private, accurate, and available.
Your child can create rules to help prevent cyberbullying.
Your child understands many things that help keep a person mentally healthy.
Your child will practice how to cover their mouth and nose properly when coughing or sneezing.
canonical_name: Grade 7 Science: Human Body Systems — Excretory and Immune Health
coherence_reason: The standards address different topics: excretory system, digital security, pest management, mental health, and hygiene.
split_topics: Human Body Systems — Excretory and Immune Health

subject: Science
sample_standards: Career Research
Your child understands how different parts of the brain have special jobs and what happens if those parts get hurt.
Your child learns about communication and teamwork skills.
Performance Expectations
Your child works well with others and values different skills and backgrounds on a team.
Your child compares the jobs and duties of different healthcare team members.
Your child can make a list of responsibilities for themselves and healthcare workers.
Your child can tell the difference between responsible and irresponsible choices when using healthcare services.
canonical_name: Grade 7 Science: Health Careers — Roles, Responsibilities, and Teamwork
coherence_reason: Most standards are about roles and teamwork in healthcare careers, with minor inclusion of brain function and general performance expectations.

subject: Science
sample_standards: Your child can write notes or explanations in their computer programs to make them easier for others to read and fix.
Your child designs changes to computers to make them easier and better for people to use.
Your child can explain why computers can do some tasks more easily than people.
Digital tools
Your child knows what technology skills are needed for jobs.
Programming Applications
Your child can describe how computers use smart behaviors like moving robots, understanding speech, and recognizing images.
Your child can explain how people and machines work together to do tasks that neither can do alone.
canonical_name: Grade 7 Science: Computing and Technology — Human-Computer Interaction and Applications
coherence_reason: The standards address related ideas about computing, technology skills, human-computer interaction, and basic programming, but span distinct subtopics such as programming, AI, digital tools, and workforce skills.

subject: Science
sample_standards: Your child can explain, using evidence, that gravity pulls objects together and that this pull depends on how heavy the objects are.
Your child can make and use a model to show how a cell works and how its parts help it do its job.
Your child can make and share arguments to show that when an object's movement energy changes, energy moves to or from it.
Your child can do an investigation to show that all living things are made of one or more cells.
Your child can compare different design ideas carefully to see which one best meets the problem's needs and limits.
Your child can use code, pictures, or tools made by others in their own programs and give credit to the original creators.
Your child understands and can talk about how technology can be unfair or hard for some people to use.
Your child can describe the world using maps and other geography tools.
canonical_name: Grade 7 Science: Multiple Topics — Mixed Content
coherence_reason: Standards span physical science (gravity, energy), life science (cells), engineering design, computer science (intellectual property, accessibility), and geography, indicating multiple unrelated major topics.
split_topics: Life Science: Cells and Cell Models
Computer Science: Reusing Code and Accessibility

subject: Science
sample_standards: Your child can design, build, test, and improve a device that either gives off or takes in heat through chemical reactions.
Your child can design, build, and test a device that either reduces or increases heat transfer.
Engineering Design
Energy
Identify that heat rises.
Developing Possible Solutions
PS1.B: Chemical Reactions
Your child uses science to design, build, and test a device that either reduces or increases heat transfer.
canonical_name: Grade 7 Science: Energy — Heat Transfer and Engineering Design
coherence_reason: Most standards focus on heat transfer and engineering design with minor inclusion of chemical reactions and general energy concepts.
